message.content: em qual pasta ficam salvas localmente as conversas do claude code?

content.thinking: O usuário está perguntando onde ficam salvas localmente as conversas do Claude Code. Vou verificar a documentação do Claude Code para responder com precisão.

content.text:
As conversas do Claude Code são salvas localmente em:

```
~/.claude/projects/
```

Dentro dessa pasta, as conversas são organizadas por projeto, usando um hash do caminho do diretório do projeto. Cada projeto tem seus próprios arquivos de conversa em formato JSON.

Você também pode encontrar outros dados do Claude Code em `~/.claude/`, incluindo:
- `settings.json` - configurações globais
- `statsig/` - dados de analytics
- `todos/` - tarefas pendentes

Para ver suas conversas, você pode explorar:

```bash
ls -la ~/.claude/projects/
```

content.content: Olá Claude! Precisamos criar uma versão final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitacao. O mais importante é impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma exceptional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Sua missão é livremente criar um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irá descrever a versão final do produto, incluindo no documento imagens de referencia - usaremos .md e você deve incluir um Moodboard com das referencias reais, o mesmo vale para as decisões de como serão todas as outras solucoes, como você não irá desenhar realmente nesta tarefa, você incluirá imagens de exemplo de como deverá ficar a tela, as imagens podem ser de telas de apps concorrentes ou outras referencias (usar , não incluir apenas links) - A fonte da verdade é a RFQ e, embora você possa ler os demais documentos no projeto, deve considerar que muita coisa neles não ficou boa durante as POCs e precisamos de um plano final - está decidido e testado que o framework final será mesmo o deck.gl e que não implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser, como zoom, pan, pitch, etc - devemos utilizar as melhores opcoes disponiveis para se trabalhar com deck.gl dentro da nossa necessidade - você deve buscar na internet, fazer extensa pesquisa e utilizar o sistema harness para savaguardar em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessão. Utilize modo planejamento e pensamento profundo. Inicie.

toolUseResult.stdout: Olá Claude! Precisamos criar uma versão final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitacao. O mais importante é impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma exceptional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Sua missão é livremente criar um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irá descrever a versão final do produto, incluindo no documento imagens de referencia - usaremos .md e você deve incluir um Moodboard com das referencias reais, o mesmo vale para as decisões de como serão todas as outras solucoes, como você não irá desenhar realmente nesta tarefa, você incluirá imagens de exemplo de como deverá ficar a tela, as imagens podem ser de telas de apps concorrentes ou outras referencias (usar , não incluir apenas links) - A fonte da verdade é a RFQ e, embora você possa ler os demais documentos no projeto, deve considerar que muita coisa neles não ficou boa durante as POCs e precisamos de um plano final - está decidido e testado que o framework final será mesmo o deck.gl e que não implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser, como zoom, pan, pitch, etc - devemos utilizar as melhores opcoes disponiveis para se trabalhar com deck.gl dentro da nossa necessidade - você deve buscar na internet, fazer extensa pesquisa e utilizar o sistema harness para savaguardar em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessão. content.content: Olá Claude! Precisamos criar uma versão final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitacao. O mais importante é impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma exceptional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). Sua missão é livremente criar um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irá descrever a versão final do produto, incluindo no documento imagens de referencia - usaremos .md e você deve incluir um Moodboard com das referencias reais, o mesmo vale para as decisões de como serão todas as outras solucoes, como você não irá desenhar realmente nesta tarefa, você incluirá imagens de exemplo de como deverá ficar a tela, as imagens podem ser de telas de apps concorrentes ou outras referencias (usar , não incluir apenas links) - A fonte da verdade é a RFQ e, embora você possa ler os demais documentos no projeto, deve considerar que muita coisa neles não ficou boa durante as POCs e precisamos de um plano final - está decidido e testado que o framework final será mesmo o deck.gl e que não implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser, como zoom, pan, pitch, etc - devemos utilizar as melhores opcoes disponiveis para se trabalhar com deck.gl dentro da nossa necessidade - você deve buscar na internet, fazer extensa pesquisa e utilizar o sistema harness para savaguardar em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessão. Utilize modo planejamento e pensamento profundo. Inicie. é importante eu esclarecer algo importante, o documento é para uso interno, a proxima tarefa será atraves do documento a implementacao do frontend iterativo com astrojs e deck.gl, utilizando mocks para os dados, portanto, o documento deve ser claro e completo com essa finalidade, não será apresentado aos clientes o documento, e sim a demo interativa. Além disso, não considerar como logicamente ou tecnicamente nenhum documento ou código no projeto com excessão de docs/RFQ.md - não usar nenhum como referencia. A tela mais importante é a de planejamento/visão dos dados com a implementacao do modo "sandbox" que servirá para resolver a solicitacao "what-if scenarios". Utilizar inglês na documentacao. Utilizar o harness. A tela mais importante é a de planejamento/visão dos dados com a implementacao do modo "sandbox" que servirá para resolver a solicitacao "what-if scenarios". Utilizar inglês na documentacao. Utilizar o harness para registrar o progresso e plano dessa task. Gostei da referencia da timeline do linear. podemos usar ele como referencia para design. continue O "mood" do design dele inteiro pode servir de referencia (espacamentos, cores, etc, mas não solucoes, por se tratar de problemas diferentes dos quais solucionaremos) É iportante que fique claramente diferenciado o que será inspirado no linear e o que será inspirado em apps de scheduling, manutencao, ferrovias, etc... para que nao seja confundido durante as secoes de implementacao. Sobre referencias, considere também ler os documentos atuais do projeto, mas apenas como complemento de pesquisa, acesse as fontes e verifique se cada dado e fonte se enquadra no novo planejamento. content.text: Aqui está a primeira mensagem reescrita com todos os ajustes incorporados: --- > Olá Claude! Precisamos criar uma versão final da parte visual do app para apresentar nossa proposta ao cliente e ganharmos o processo de licitação. O mais importante é impressionarmos o cliente visualmente e interativamente com nosso frontend, de modo a demonstrar que podemos solucionar de forma excepcional as dores que ele reportou na RFQ (./docs/RFQ.md). > > **Objetivo:** Sua missão é criar livremente um documento de UX/Design/Produto que irá descrever a versão final do produto. Este documento é para **uso interno** - não será apresentado aos clientes. A próxima tarefa será, através deste documento, a implementação do frontend interativo com **Astro.js e deck.gl**, utilizando mocks para os dados. Portanto, o documento deve ser claro e completo com essa finalidade. > > **Formato do documento:** > - Usar formato .md com o documento escrito em **inglês** > - Incluir um Moodboard com `` das referências reais (não apenas links) > - Para cada decisão de tela/solução, incluir imagens de exemplo de como deverá ficar, usando screenshots de apps concorrentes ou outras referências > > **Fontes e Referências:** > - A **fonte da verdade é exclusivamente a RFQ** (docs/RFQ.md) - não considerar logicamente ou tecnicamente nenhum outro documento ou código no projeto > - Os demais documentos do projeto podem ser lidos apenas como **complemento de pesquisa** - acessar as fontes originais e verificar se cada dado se enquadra no novo planejamento > - Fazer extensa pesquisa na internet para referências visuais e soluções > > **Referências de Design:** > - Usar o **Linear** como referência principal de design, especialmente a timeline > - O "mood" do Linear (espaçamentos, cores, tipografia) serve de referência geral, mas **não as soluções**, por se tratar de problemas diferentes > - **Importante:** Diferenciar claramente no documento o que é inspirado no Linear vs. o que é inspirado em apps de scheduling, manutenção, ferrovias, etc. - para evitar confusão durante as seções de implementação > > **Tela Principal:** > - A tela mais importante é a de **planejamento/visão dos dados** com a implementação do modo **"sandbox"** que servirá para resolver a solicitação de "what-if scenarios" > > **Tecnologia:** > - Está decidido e testado que o framework final será **deck.gl** > - Não implementaremos manualmente features que podem causar problemas cross-browser (zoom, pan, pitch, etc.) - devemos utilizar as melhores opções disponíveis para trabalhar com deck.gl > > **Harness:** > - Utilizar o sistema harness para salvaguardar progresso em caso de necessidade de continuarmos em nova sessão > - Registrar o progresso e plano dessa task no harness > > Utilize modo planejamento e pensamento profundo. Inicie. --- Quer que eu salve isso em algum arquivo?

Também há um `docs/archive` que preciso verificar se existe. Vou:
1. Ler os arquivos atuais para entender a estrutura
2. Criar backup dos documentos atuais em docs/archive
3. Reiniciar os arquivos de progresso e features com a nova tarefa

Vou primeiro verificar a estrutura atual e depois criar os novos arquivos.

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.5.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-17",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + deck.gl + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "migration": {
    "status": "COMPLETE_MIGRATION_REQUIRED",
    "from": "React Three Fiber (R3F) + Three.js",
    "to": "deck.gl with OrthographicView (ONLY)",
    "reason": "docs/deck-research/mms-deck-research.md",
    "archive": "docs/archive/r3f-architecture.md",
    "note": "CRITICAL: Current implementation has BOTH R3F and deck.gl. R3F is DEFAULT. Must completely remove R3F and make deck.gl the ONLY rendering system."
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Complete R3F Removal - Delete ALL Three.js/R3F code and dependencies",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Removed all R3F/Three.js imports, deleted canvas/, rendering/, spatial/ directories, removed dependencies. App renders with deck.gl only at 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": null,
      "rationale": "Current implementation has BOTH R3F and deck.gl with R3F as default. This defeats the purpose of migration. Must completely remove R3F to achieve the promised benefits: smaller bundle, simpler architecture, better 2D performance.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Update App.tsx to ONLY use DeckCanvas (remove useDeckGL toggle, remove InfiniteCanvas import)",
        "2. Delete entire apps/canvas/src/canvas/ directory (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + layers/)",
        "3. Delete apps/canvas/src/rendering/ directory (R3F-specific hooks)",
        "4. Remove R3F dependencies: bun remove @react-three/fiber @react-three/drei three @types/three r3f-perf troika-three-utils",
        "5. Update @mms/toolkit state: clean up any R3F-specific state in viewport.ts",
        "6. Remove spatial index R3F integration from apps/canvas/src/spatial/",
        "7. Verify no imports reference deleted files",
        "8. Run typecheck and fix any errors"
      ],
      "successCriteria": [
        "No @react-three/* imports in codebase",
        "No three.js imports in codebase",
        "Bundle size reduced by ~200KB",
        "App renders with deck.gl only",
        "All features work: pan, zoom, LOD transitions, hover, click, sidebar/timeline sync"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-002",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Implement @use-gesture/react for cross-platform input handling",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Created useCanvasGestures hook with pinch/wheel/drag handlers. Integrated into DeckCanvas, disabled deck.gl controller for pan/zoom. Trackpad detection, delta clamping implemented. Visual tests pass - all LOD transitions work, 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "rationale": "Research doc (mms-deck-research.md) specifically recommends @use-gesture/react for cross-platform input normalization. It's already installed but NOT used. deck.gl's built-in controller has limitations with Safari trackpad and touch gestures.",
      "research": "docs/deck-research/mms-deck-research.md - Section: 'The recommended solution: @use-gesture/react'",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Create apps/canvas/src/deck/hooks/useCanvasGestures.ts",
        "2. Implement useGesture with onPinch, onWheel, onDrag handlers",
        "3. Add Safari GestureEvent handling (real scale/rotation props)",
        "4. Add trackpad vs mouse detection from event frequency",
        "5. Add delta clamping (MAX_DELTA=50) to prevent wild jumps",
        "6. Integrate with DeckCanvas - replace deck.gl's built-in controller for zoom/pan",
        "7. Add momentum panning (velocity-based) if time permits",
        "8. Test on: Chrome Mac, Safari Mac, Chrome Windows, Firefox"
      ],
      "configuration": {
        "onPinch": "({ offset: [scale], origin: [ox, oy] }) => zoomAtPoint(scale, ox, oy)",
        "onWheel": "({ delta: [dx, dy], ctrlKey }) => ctrlKey handled by onPinch, else pan(-dx, -dy)",
        "onDrag": "({ delta: [dx, dy] }) => pan(dx, dy)",
        "eventOptions": "{ passive: false } // Required for preventDefault"
      },
      "successCriteria": [
        "Trackpad pinch zoom is smooth on Safari Mac",
        "Two-finger scroll pans (not zooms) on all platforms",
        "Mouse wheel zooms toward cursor",
        "Ctrl+wheel zooms (handled by pinch)",
        "No browser zoom triggered (preventDefault working)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-003",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Implement RBush spatial index for viewport culling",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Installed rbush, created RBushSpatialIndex.ts with bulkLoad and getVisibleTasks methods. Integrated into DeckCanvas - spatial index initialized on mount, visibleTasks used for MESO/MICRO layers. All LOD levels verified: MACRO 120fps, MESO 120fps, MICRO 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "rationale": "Research doc recommends RBush R-tree for O(log n) spatial queries. Current SimpleSpatialIndex is O(n) placeholder. With 60k tasks, this matters for performance.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Install RBush: bun add rbush @types/rbush",
        "2. Create apps/canvas/src/deck/spatial/RBushSpatialIndex.ts implementing ISpatialIndex",
        "3. Implement bulk load on startup (tree.load() for all tasks)",
        "4. Implement viewport query with overscan margin",
        "5. Update DeckCanvas to use RBush for visible task calculation",
        "6. Pass visible task IDs to layers instead of full task array",
        "7. Layers filter their data using visible IDs",
        "8. Benchmark: viewport query <5ms for 1000 results"
      ],
      "successCriteria": [
        "Viewport query time <5ms",
        "Only visible tasks passed to layers",
        "60fps maintained during rapid pan/zoom",
        "No visible pop-in of tasks"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-004",
      "category": "ui",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Sidebar and Timeline alignment with deck.gl canvas",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Verified alignment at all LOD levels (MACRO 0.44x, MESO 1.00x, MICRO 2.25x). Sidebar rows scale with zoom (rowHeight = pxPerTrain * zoom), timeline syncs with viewportLeftX/rightX, navigation works correctly. 120fps maintained.",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "rationale": "User requested proper alignment between sidebar train labels and canvas rows. The left sidebar should match the canvas rows exactly like a Gantt chart. Timeline header should sync with horizontal scroll.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Verify sidebar row height matches deck.gl pxPerTrain constant",
        "2. Sync sidebar scroll position with canvas viewState.target[1] (Y coordinate)",
        "3. Verify timeline header syncs with viewState.target[0] (X coordinate)",
        "4. Ensure alternating row backgrounds in sidebar match canvas row backgrounds",
        "5. Add visual connector between sidebar and canvas (shared row background color)",
        "6. Test alignment at all zoom levels (MACRO, MESO, MICRO)",
        "7. Fix any misalignment issues"
      ],
      "note": "Research confirmed: deck.gl doesn't have built-in sidebar/timeline components. Use CSS-positioned React components alongside DeckGL canvas. This is the standard approach.",
      "successCriteria": [

content.content: # Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log ## Session: 2025-12-19 (DESIGN_SPEC.md Creation) - IN PROGRESS 🔄 ### Task: Create Implementation Design Specification Document **Objective:** Create `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` - an internal technical specification document that will guide the implementation of the demo with Astro.js + deck.gl. Focus on the Planning View with Sandbox Mode for what-if scenarios. **Key Decisions:** - Source of truth: ONLY `docs/RFQ.md` (ignore existing code/docs) - Stack: Astro.js + deck.gl + mocks - Language: English - Primary feature: Planning View with Sandbox Mode (what-if scenarios) - Theme: Dark mode enterprise **RFQ Pain Points to Solve:** 1. No dedicated mileage management tools 2. IBM Maximo only does asset management 3. Manual Excel management 4. Folder-driven data storage 5. Limited maintenance-operations integration 6. Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) 7. No predictive simulations 8. Insufficient resource visibility 9. Manual handling delays **Progress:** - ✅ Research: deck.gl, what-if scenario patterns, dashboard design - ✅ Collected visual references (Kepler.gl, Linear, Figma templates) - ✅ Created `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` with complete specifications - ✅ Detailed Sandbox Mode (what-if scenarios) as primary feature - ✅ Added moodboard with `` tags for visual references **Document Created: `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md`** Contents: 1. Visual Mood & Design Philosophy (Linear as reference) 2. Moodboard with `` tags (Kepler.gl, Flowmap, SandDance, Figma templates, Railfleet) 3. Problem → Solution Matrix (all 9 RFQ pain points) 4. Design System (colors, typography, spacing, radius) 5. Screen Specifications (Application Shell, Navbar, Sidebar) 6. Planning View - Semantic Zoom (MACRO/MESO/MICRO) 7. **Sandbox Mode** (PRIMARY FEATURE) - complete specification - Mode Toggle UI - Visual Differentiation (violet accent) - Sandbox Panel with delta metrics - Sandbox Interactions (drag, resize, create, delete) - Compare View (baseline vs scenario) - Saved Scenarios management 8. Components Specification (TaskBlock, KPICard, AlertBanner, Minimap) 9. Data Structures (Train, Task, Scenario entities) 10. Interactions & Gestures 11. Performance Targets 12. Implementation Stack (Astro.js, deck.gl, XState, Tailwind) 13. File Structure (proposed) 14. RFQ Requirements Mapping **Status: COMPLETE ✅** --- ## Session: 2025-12-18 (Semantic Zoom POC) - COMPLETE ✅ ### Task: Complete Semantic Zoom transitions POC with crossfade **Objective:** Test and verify the Semantic Zoom POC that implements smooth crossfade transitions between 4 LOD levels using Hermite smoothstep interpolation. **Completed:** 1. **Semantic Zoom POC Story:** - `apps/canvas/src/stories/01-POCs/Rendering/SemanticZoom.stories.tsx` - 4 LOD levels: Strategic (<0.3x), Tactical (0.3-1.0x), Operational (1.0-3.0x), Execution (>3.0x) - Crossfade transitions with smoothstep Hermite interpolation - Layer opacities panel showing real-time opacity percentages - Interactive zoom slider with threshold markers 2. **LOD Level Visualizations:** - **Strategic (0.15x):** Blue heatmap with percentage cells (50%, 65%, 78%, etc.) - **Tactical (0.60x):** Train IDs list (T-001 to T-016) with quarterly bars label - **Operational (2.00x):** DNA Gantt view with orange styling - **Execution (4.00x):** Detailed task cards with train ID, task type (PM1/PM2/PM3/Overhaul), status badges (completed/in-progress/scheduled) 3. **Crossfade Transitions Verified:** - Strategic→Tactical (at 0.3): Tested at 0.34x - strategic: 30%, tactical: 70% - Tactical→Operational (at 1.0): Tested at 0.94x - tactical: 78%, operational: 22% - Operational→Execution (at 3.0): Tested at 2.94x - operational: 78%, execution: 22% - All transitions use smooth Hermite interpolation (no sudden jumps) 4. **Story Variants Created:** - Default: Interactive slider for testing all transitions - At Strategic Level: Pre-set to 0.15x - At Tactical Level: Pre-set to 0.60x - At Operational Level: Pre-set to 2.00x - At Execution Level: Pre-set to 4.00x **Testing Results:** - All 4 LOD levels render correctly with distinct visuals - Crossfade transitions are smooth and use correct opacity blending

This document is NOT for client presentation - the interactive demo built from it will be presented instead. **Key Requirements:** 1. **Document Purpose:** Internal technical specification for Astro.js + deck.gl implementation with mocks 2. **Language:** English 3. **Format:** Markdown (.md) with embedded `` tags for visual references (not just links) 4. **Source of Truth:** ONLY `docs/RFQ.md` - ignore all other existing documents/code as technical reference 5. **Existing Docs:** Read only as research complement - verify each source fits the new plan **Primary Feature Focus:** - **Planning View with Sandbox Mode** - the most important screen - Sandbox Mode enables "what-if scenarios" as requested in RFQ **Design References:** - **Linear App** - use as primary design reference (timeline, mood, spacing, colors, typography) - Clearly differentiate what is inspired by Linear vs. scheduling/maintenance/railway apps - Linear provides aesthetic inspiration, NOT solutions (different problem domain) **Technology Constraints:** - Framework: deck.gl (confirmed and tested) - Do NOT implement manual cross-browser features (zoom, pan, pitch) - use deck.gl built-in capabilities - Use best available options for working with deck.gl **Document Contents:** - Moodboard with `` references - Screen specifications with visual examples - Component specifications - Interaction patterns - Data structures - Implementation guidelines ### Sub-Tasks 1. [ ] Backup current docs to `docs/archive/` 2. [ ] Research: deck.gl patterns, what-if scenarios, dashboard design 3. [ ] Collect visual references (Linear, Kepler.gl, railway apps, scheduling apps) 4. [ ] Create `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` with: - [ ] Visual Mood & Design Philosophy - [ ] Moodboard with `` tags - [ ] Problem -> Solution Matrix (RFQ pain points) - [ ] Design System (colors, typography, spacing) - [ ] Screen Specifications - [ ] Planning View with Sandbox Mode (PRIMARY) - [ ] Component Specifications - [ ] Data Structures - [ ] Interactions & Gestures - [ ] Implementation Stack ### RFQ Pain Points to Address From `docs/RFQ.md`: 1. No dedicated mileage management tools 2. IBM Maximo only does asset management 3. Manual Excel management 4. Folder-driven data storage 5. Limited maintenance-operations integration 6. Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) 7. No predictive simulations 8. Insufficient resource visibility 9. Manual handling delays ### Notes - Previous implementation attempts and POCs should be ignored - Start fresh based solely on RFQ requirements - Focus on impressing the client visually and interactively --- ## Archive Note Previous progress and features have been archived to: - `docs/archive/features.json.bak-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` - `docs/archive/claude-progress.txt.bak-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS` The project is resetting focus to the DESIGN_PLAN task as the new starting point.

input.content: # Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log ## Current Task: DESIGN_PLAN - UX/Design/Product Document Creation ### Status: IN PROGRESS ### Objective Create the final visual/UX/Design/Product document (`docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md`) to guide the implementation of the demo frontend with Astro.js + deck.gl. ### Task Description (Consolidated from DESIGN_PLAN session) Create a comprehensive UX/Design/Product document that will serve as the internal specification for implementing the demo. This document is NOT for client presentation - the interactive demo built from it will be presented instead. **Key Requirements:** 1. **Document Purpose:** Internal technical specification for Astro.js + deck.gl implementation with mocks 2. **Language:** English 3. **Format:** Markdown (.md) with embedded `` tags for visual references (not just links) 4. **Source of Truth:** ONLY `docs/RFQ.md` - ignore all other existing documents/code as technical reference 5. **Existing Docs:** Read only as research complement - verify each source fits the new plan **Primary Feature Focus:** - **Planning View with Sandbox Mode** - the most important screen - Sandbox Mode enables "what-if scenarios" as requested in RFQ **Design References:** - **Linear App** - use as primary design reference (timeline, mood, spacing, colors, typography) - Clearly differentiate what is inspired by Linear vs. scheduling/maintenance/railway apps - Linear provides aesthetic inspiration, NOT solutions (different problem domain) **Technology Constraints:** - Framework: deck.gl (confirmed and tested) - Do NOT implement manual cross-browser features (zoom, pan, pitch) - use deck.gl built-in capabilities - Use best available options for working with deck.gl **Document Contents:** - Moodboard with `` references - Screen specifications with visual examples - Component specifications - Interaction patterns - Data structures - Implementation guidelines ### Sub-Tasks 1. [ ] Backup current docs to `docs/archive/` 2. [ ] Research: deck.gl patterns, what-if scenarios, dashboard design 3. [ ] Collect visual references (Linear, Kepler.gl, railway apps, scheduling apps) 4. [ ] Create `docs/DESIGN_SPEC.md` with: - [ ] Visual Mood & Design Philosophy - [ ] Moodboard with `` tags - [ ] Problem -> Solution Matrix (RFQ pain points) - [ ] Design System (colors, typography, spacing) - [ ] Screen Specifications - [ ] Planning View with Sandbox Mode (PRIMARY) - [ ] Component Specifications - [ ] Data Structures - [ ] Interactions & Gestures - [ ] Implementation Stack ### RFQ Pain Points to Address From `docs/RFQ.md`: 1. No dedicated mileage management tools 2. IBM Maximo only does asset management 3. Manual Excel management 4. Folder-driven data storage 5. Limited maintenance-operations integration 6. Non-compliance risk (underrun/overrun) 7. No predictive simulations 8. Insufficient resource visibility 9. Manual handling delays ### Notes - Previous implementation attempts and POCs should be ignored - Start fresh based solely on RFQ requirements - Focus on impressing the client visually and interactively --- ## Archive Note Previous progress and features have been archived to `docs/archive/` with timestamps. The project is resetting focus to the DESIGN_PLAN task as the new starting point.

-- uPlot: Best raw performance for massive datasets
-- deck.gl DataFilterExtension: Best for GPU-accelerated filtering
-- LTTB: Essential for overview charts (4383 → 100 points = 43.8x compression)
-- BlueprintJS RangeSlider: Best for UI consistency with existing components

Read: `docs/state-management-research/relatorio-state-management.pdf` (11 pages) - Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` (railway scheduling - not relevant) - Read: `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (current LOD implementation) **Key Findings:** 1. **InstancedMesh Problem:** Three.js sends ALL instances to GPU regardless of viewport - 60k instances = 60k transforms uploaded every frame - No built-in per-instance frustum culling 2. **RBush Spatial Index:** - Bulk load 1M items ~1600ms (one-time) - Viewport query for 1000 results ~4ms - Perfect for viewport culling queries 3. **Culling Strategy:** - Reorder visible instances to front of buffer - Set `mesh.count = visibleCount` (only visible rendered) - No new allocations during pan/zoom 4. **Alternative Libraries:** - `@three.ez/instanced-mesh` (InstancedMesh2) - has BVH + per-instance culling - `three-mesh-bvh` - accelerated raycasting - `flatbush` - 5-8x faster than RBush for static data **Documents Created:** - LINE mode (deltaMode=1): multiply by LINE_HEIGHT (40px) - PAGE mode (deltaMode=2): multiply by PAGE_HEIGHT (800px) - PIXEL mode (deltaMode=0): use directly 2. **Trackpad vs Mouse Detection:** - Track event frequency with timestamps - Events < 20ms apart = trackpad behavior - After 3+ rapid events, mark as trackpad - Large deltas (>50px) reset to mouse mode 3. **Adaptive Sensitivity:** - Mouse: MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003 - Trackpad: TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 (lower for many small events) 4. **Zoom Delta Clamping:** - MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5 - Prevents jump zoom from large scroll events **Constants Added:** ```typescript LINE_HEIGHT = 40 // Pixels per line PAGE_HEIGHT = 800 // Pixels per page MOUSE_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.003 // Base zoom sensitivity TRACKPAD_ZOOM_SENSITIVITY = 0.001 // Lower for trackpad MAX_ZOOM_DELTA_PER_EVENT = 0.5 // Cap per event TRACKPAD_DETECTION_THRESHOLD = 20 // ms between events TRACKPAD_EVENT_COUNT_THRESHOLD = 3 // Rapid events to confirm ``` **Files Changed:** - Pan/zoom navigation with cursor-centered zoom - Momentum physics for inertial panning - Pinch-to-zoom gesture support ### Phase 3: LOD Layers (F107-F110) - Three LOD levels: MACRO (heatmap), MESO (instanced blocks), MICRO (HTML cards) - Smooth crossfade transitions between layers - Frustum culling for performance ### Phase 4: UI Components (F111-F115) - Sidebar with train IDs synced to viewport - Timeline with adaptive date labels - Navbar with zoom controls and LOD indicator - Instructions toaster with auto-dismiss ### Phase 5: Polish (F116-F124) - Performance optimization (60fps target) - Cross-platform scroll normalization - Elastic jump fix (cameraPosRef workaround) - Interactive sidebar/timeline navigation ### Harness Improvements - Legacy code cleanup (removed React 19/Astro) - QA protocol with Playwright MCP - Visual testing mandatory for UI features ### Known Issues Resolved - R3F 8.x/9.x version mismatch with React 18 - BlueprintJS Dark theme incompatibility - Wheel event deltaMode normalization - Stale closure in animation loops (F122) For full session details, see git history. --- ## Session: 2025-12-18 (Complete Storybook Demo) - COMPLETE ✅ ### Task: Complete all 4 LOD layers and FractalCanvas demo **Objective:** Complete the remaining LOD layer implementations and create a unified FractalCanvas demo with semantic zoom transitions. **Completed:** 1. **Execution Layer (Task 13):** - `apps/canvas/src/stories/02-Layers/Execution/ExecutionLayer.tsx` - Detailed Gantt view with BlueprintJS Card components - Task cards showing: name, status, dates, progress bar, resources (hours, man-hours, pit) - Conflict indicators with warning icons - Fixed positioning issues (ROW_HEIGHT * zoom → fixed ROW_HEIGHT) - 5 story variants: Default, WithControls, StatusFilter, WeekView, ResourceView 2. **FractalCanvas Integration (Tasks 14-15):** - `apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/FractalCanvas.tsx` - `apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/FractalCanvas.stories.tsx` - Unified component integrating all 4 LOD layers with crossfade - LOD levels: Strategic (<0.3x), Tactical (0.3-1.0x), Operational (1.0-3.0x), Execution (>3.0x) - Smooth Hermite smoothstep transitions between layers - Sidebar showing train IDs synced with viewport - Layer visibility panel showing real-time opacity percentages - 7 story variants including TransitionDemo and ZoomRangeExplorer **Layer Summary:** | Layer | Zoom | Visualization | Technology | |-------|------|---------------|------------| | Strategic | < 0.3x | 12-year heatmap (yellow→orange→red) | deck.gl SolidPolygonLayer | | Tactical | 0.3-1.0x | Monthly stacked bars | deck.gl SolidPolygonLayer | | Operational | 1.0-3.0x | DNA-style task blocks | deck.gl SolidPolygonLayer | | Execution | > 3.0x | Detailed task cards | HTML/CSS with BlueprintJS | **Testing Results:** - All 4 LOD layers render correctly - Crossfade transitions are smooth (15% overlap range) - Sidebar syncs with viewport camera position - No console errors - 128 trains, 37,200 tasks generated correctly **Run Demo:** ```bash cd apps/canvas && bun run storybook # Navigate to 03-Demo/FractalCanvas ``` **All Tasks Completed:** 1. ✅ Update plan with user decisions (XState, BlueprintJS, no dark mode) 2. ✅ Setup folder structure (01-POCs, 02-Layers, 03-Demo) 3. ✅ Install XState 5.x dependencies 4. ✅ Create mock data generator with 128 trains, 12 years, 60K tasks 5. ✅ POC: XState viewport machine (zoom, pan, LOD states) 6. ✅ POC: deck.gl heatmap for Strategic view 7. ✅ POC: Gesture recognizer (pan, pinch, momentum) 8. ✅ POC: Semantic zoom transitions (crossfade) 9. ✅ Components: GanttBar, KPICard, TodayLine, TimelineHeader 10. ✅ Layer: Strategic (12-year heatmap) 11. ✅ Layer: Tactical (stacked bars) 12. ✅ Layer: Operational (DNA Gantt) 13. ✅ Layer: Execution (detailed Gantt with resources) 14. ✅ Integration: Semantic zoom + sidebar/timeline sync 15. ✅ Demo: FractalCanvas.stories.tsx final **Project Status: DEMO COMPLETE** --- ## Session: 2025-12-18 (FractalCanvasComplete with streetscape.gl-style Features) - COMPLETE ✅ ### Task: Product Plan & Complete Demo Implementation **Objective:** Based on research documents (not architecture docs), create a comprehensive product plan addressing client pain points from RFQ, then implement a complete demo with streetscape.gl-style visualization features. **Research Phase:** - Read: `docs/RESEARCH.md` - Railway scheduling best practices (Tokyo Metro "Equalizing") - Read: `docs/UX_RESEARCH.md` - Pan/zoom patterns (Figma, Maps, iOS) - Read: `docs/VIEWPORT_CULLING_RESEARCH.md` - Performance patterns - Read: `docs/mms-deck-research.md` - deck.gl visualization patterns - Read: `docs/mms-components-research.md` - Component architecture - Searched: streetscape.gl documentation (Uber's visualization framework built on deck.gl) **Client Pain Points Identified (from RFQ):** 1. No dedicated tools for mileage management (Excel-based) 2. IBM Maximo is asset management only, not mileage-specific 3. Raw mileage data stored manually (folder-driven) 4. Limited integration between maintenance and operations 5. Risk of non-compliance due to manual mileage tracking 6. No predictive simulations 7. Insufficient resource visibility vs utilization 8. Manual handling causes delays and inefficiencies 9. No short-term/long-term planning integration 10. No "What-If" scenario capabilities **Product Plan Created:** - `docs/PRODUCT_PLAN.md` - Comprehensive product architecture based on research - 4 LOD levels with specific zoom ranges and visualizations - Component architecture with deck.gl layers - TimeScrubber with LTTB downsampling - ConstraintDeck with resource metrics - Implementation phases defined **Components Implemented:** 1. **ConstraintDeck (`apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/ConstraintDeck.tsx`):** - Resource constraint visualization panel - 4 metrics: Man-Hours, Track Occupancy, Budget, Compliance - BlueprintJS ProgressBar with Intent colors (SUCCESS/WARNING/DANGER) - Collapsible panel with header - Default props for demo data 2. **TimeScrubber (`apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/TimeScrubber.tsx`):** - Time range selector with Canvas-based density visualization - LTTB (Largest Triangle Three Buckets) downsampling for performance - BlueprintJS RangeSlider for selection handles - Stats display: total tasks, average per period - Gradient fill chart with selection highlighting 3. **FractalCanvasComplete (`apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/FractalCanvasComplete.tsx`):** - Complete integration of all visualization components - 4 LOD layers with deck.gl SolidPolygonLayer: - Strategic (< 0.3x): 12-year heatmap with year labels - Tactical (0.3-1.0x): Monthly stacked bars with quarter labels - Operational (1.0-3.0x): DNA-style task blocks with month labels - Execution (> 3.0x): Detailed task cards with week labels - Hermite smoothstep crossfade transitions (15% overlap) - Sidebar with 128 trains (Kinki Sharyo + Alstom) - Adaptive Timeline Header (years → quarters → months → weeks) - Layer Visibility panel with real-time percentages - Zoom slider with LOD range indicators - Keyboard shortcuts (1-4 for LOD levels, +/- for zoom, Home for reset) 4. **Stories (`apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/FractalCanvasComplete.stories.tsx`):** - Default: Complete demo with all features - Strategic Level: 0.2x zoom, 12-year view - Tactical Level: 0.6x zoom, quarterly view - Operational Level: 1.8x zoom, monthly view - Execution Level: 4.0x zoom, weekly view with task cards - Minimal: No panels (TimeScrubber, ConstraintDeck hidden) - Interactive Controls: External control bar with switches - LOD Transition Demo: Auto-play animation through zoom levels - Presentation Mode: Slide-based presentation with navigation **Visual Testing Results:** - ✅ Strategic view (0.20x): Years 2024-2036, layer visibility 67% - ✅ Tactical view (0.50x): Q1 2024 - Q4 2030, layer visibility 100% - ✅ Operational view (1.80x): Months Jan 2024 - Nov 2025, layer visibility 100% - ✅ Execution view (4.00x): Weeks W53 - W45, detailed task cards with: - Task types: Corrective Maintenance, PM1 - Light Inspection, PM2 - Medium Service - Status badges: conflict, overdue, completed - Resource details: duration (2h-8h), man-hours (4-16mh), pit assignments - Conflict indicators (!) - ✅ Zoom buttons (S/T/O/E) switch LOD levels correctly - ✅ Timeline Header adapts granularity per zoom level - ✅ TimeScrubber shows density chart with selection range - ✅ ConstraintDeck shows resource metrics with progress bars - ✅ No console errors **Tech Stack Used:** - deck.gl 9.2.5 with OrthographicView - BlueprintJS 6.1.0 (Card, ProgressBar, Tag, Slider, RangeSlider, Button, Navbar) - React 18.3.1 - Canvas API for density chart (performance) - LTTB algorithm for downsampling **Data Generated:** - 128 trains (49 Kinki Sharyo + 79 Alstom) - 12-year period (2024-2036) - 37,200 maintenance tasks - ~5% conflict rate for demo **Run Demo:** ```bash cd apps/canvas && bun run storybook # Navigate to 03-Demo/FractalCanvasComplete ``` **Files Created:** - `docs/PRODUCT_PLAN.md` - Comprehensive product plan - `apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/ConstraintDeck.tsx` - Resource panel component - `apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/TimeScrubber.tsx` - Time range selector - `apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/FractalCanvasComplete.tsx` - Complete integration - `apps/canvas/src/stories/03-Demo/FractalCanvasComplete.stories.tsx` - 9 story variants **Project Status: COMPLETE DEMO WITH STREETSCAPE.GL-STYLE FEATURES** The demo now provides: 1. "Single Pane of Glass" visualization from 12-year strategic view to daily execution 2. Semantic zoom with smooth crossfade transitions 3. Resource constraint visibility (Man-Hours, Tracks, Budget, Compliance) 4. Time range selection with density visualization 5. All 4 planning horizons: Strategic, Tactical, Operational, Execution

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.5.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-17",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + deck.gl + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "migration": {
    "status": "COMPLETE_MIGRATION_REQUIRED",
    "from": "React Three Fiber (R3F) + Three.js",
    "to": "deck.gl with OrthographicView (ONLY)",
    "reason": "docs/deck-research/mms-deck-research.md",
    "archive": "docs/archive/r3f-architecture.md",
    "note": "CRITICAL: Current implementation has BOTH R3F and deck.gl. R3F is DEFAULT. Must completely remove R3F and make deck.gl the ONLY rendering system."
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Complete R3F Removal - Delete ALL Three.js/R3F code and dependencies",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Removed all R3F/Three.js imports, deleted canvas/, rendering/, spatial/ directories, removed dependencies. App renders with deck.gl only at 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": null,
      "rationale": "Current implementation has BOTH R3F and deck.gl with R3F as default. This defeats the purpose of migration. Must completely remove R3F to achieve the promised benefits: smaller bundle, simpler architecture, better 2D performance.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Update App.tsx to ONLY use DeckCanvas (remove useDeckGL toggle, remove InfiniteCanvas import)",
        "2. Delete entire apps/canvas/src/canvas/ directory (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + layers/)",
        "3. Delete apps/canvas/src/rendering/ directory (R3F-specific hooks)",
        "4. Remove R3F dependencies: bun remove @react-three/fiber @react-three/drei three @types/three r3f-perf troika-three-utils",
        "5. Update @mms/toolkit state: clean up any R3F-specific state in viewport.ts",
        "6. Remove spatial index R3F integration from apps/canvas/src/spatial/",
        "7. Verify no imports reference deleted files",
        "8. Run typecheck and fix any errors"
      ],
      "successCriteria": [
        "No @react-three/* imports in codebase",
        "No three.js imports in codebase",
        "Bundle size reduced by ~200KB",
        "App renders with deck.gl only",
        "All features work: pan, zoom, LOD transitions, hover, click, sidebar/timeline sync"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-002",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Implement @use-gesture/react for cross-platform input handling",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Created useCanvasGestures hook with pinch/wheel/drag handlers. Integrated into DeckCanvas, disabled deck.gl controller for pan/zoom. Trackpad detection, delta clamping implemented. Visual tests pass - all LOD transitions work, 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "rationale": "Research doc (mms-deck-research.md) specifically recommends @use-gesture/react for cross-platform input normalization. It's already installed but NOT used. deck.gl's built-in controller has limitations with Safari trackpad and touch gestures.",
      "research": "docs/deck-research/mms-deck-research.md - Section: 'The recommended solution: @use-gesture/react'",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Create apps/canvas/src/deck/hooks/useCanvasGestures.ts",
        "2. Implement useGesture with onPinch, onWheel, onDrag handlers",
        "3. Add Safari GestureEvent handling (real scale/rotation props)",
        "4. Add trackpad vs mouse detection from event frequency",
        "5. Add delta clamping (MAX_DELTA=50) to prevent wild jumps",
        "6. Integrate with DeckCanvas - replace deck.gl's built-in controller for zoom/pan",
        "7. Add momentum panning (velocity-based) if time permits",
        "8. Test on: Chrome Mac, Safari Mac, Chrome Windows, Firefox"
      ],
      "configuration": {
        "onPinch": "({ offset: [scale], origin: [ox, oy] }) => zoomAtPoint(scale, ox, oy)",
        "onWheel": "({ delta: [dx, dy], ctrlKey }) => ctrlKey handled by onPinch, else pan(-dx, -dy)",
        "onDrag": "({ delta: [dx, dy] }) => pan(dx, dy)",
        "eventOptions": "{ passive: false } // Required for preventDefault"
      },
      "successCriteria": [
        "Trackpad pinch zoom is smooth on Safari Mac",
        "Two-finger scroll pans (not zooms) on all platforms",
        "Mouse wheel zooms toward cursor",
        "Ctrl+wheel zooms (handled by pinch)",
        "No browser zoom triggered (preventDefault working)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-003",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Implement RBush spatial index for viewport culling",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Installed rbush, created RBushSpatialIndex.ts with bulkLoad and getVisibleTasks methods. Integrated into DeckCanvas - spatial index initialized on mount, visibleTasks used for MESO/MICRO layers. All LOD levels verified: MACRO 120fps, MESO 120fps, MICRO 120fps.",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "rationale": "Research doc recommends RBush R-tree for O(log n) spatial queries. Current SimpleSpatialIndex is O(n) placeholder. With 60k tasks, this matters for performance.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Install RBush: bun add rbush @types/rbush",
        "2. Create apps/canvas/src/deck/spatial/RBushSpatialIndex.ts implementing ISpatialIndex",
        "3. Implement bulk load on startup (tree.load() for all tasks)",
        "4. Implement viewport query with overscan margin",
        "5. Update DeckCanvas to use RBush for visible task calculation",
        "6. Pass visible task IDs to layers instead of full task array",
        "7. Layers filter their data using visible IDs",
        "8. Benchmark: viewport query <5ms for 1000 results"
      ],
      "successCriteria": [
        "Viewport query time <5ms",
        "Only visible tasks passed to layers",
        "60fps maintained during rapid pan/zoom",
        "No visible pop-in of tasks"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-004",
      "category": "ui",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Sidebar and Timeline alignment with deck.gl canvas",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Verified alignment at all LOD levels (MACRO 0.44x, MESO 1.00x, MICRO 2.25x). Sidebar rows scale with zoom (rowHeight = pxPerTrain * zoom), timeline syncs with viewportLeftX/rightX, navigation works correctly. 120fps maintained.",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "rationale": "User requested proper alignment between sidebar train labels and canvas rows. The left sidebar should match the canvas rows exactly like a Gantt chart. Timeline header should sync with horizontal scroll.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Verify sidebar row height matches deck.gl pxPerTrain constant",
        "2. Sync sidebar scroll position with canvas viewState.target[1] (Y coordinate)",
        "3. Verify timeline header syncs with viewState.target[0] (X coordinate)",
        "4. Ensure alternating row backgrounds in sidebar match canvas row backgrounds",
        "5. Add visual connector between sidebar and canvas (shared row background color)",
        "6. Test alignment at all zoom levels (MACRO, MESO, MICRO)",
        "7. Fix any misalignment issues"
      ],
      "note": "Research confirmed: deck.gl doesn't have built-in sidebar/timeline components. Use CSS-positioned React components alongside DeckGL canvas. This is the standard approach.",
      "successCriteria": [
        "Sidebar row N exactly aligned with canvas row N at all zoom levels",
        "Timeline header date labels sync perfectly with canvas X position",
        "No visual disconnect between sidebar and canvas",
        "Smooth scrolling sync during pan"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-COMPLETE-005",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Performance validation and bundle optimization",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Bundle size 356KB gzipped (main chunk) - under 500KB target. FPS: 120fps (avg) at all LOD levels during idle and interaction. All features working: pan, zoom, LOD transitions, hover, click, sidebar/timeline sync. Note: Mobile testing deferred, pre-existing TypeScript errors need cleanup but don't affect runtime.",
      "blockedBy": ["DECK-COMPLETE-001", "DECK-COMPLETE-002", "DECK-COMPLETE-003", "DECK-COMPLETE-004"],
      "rationale": "Final validation that migration achieved promised benefits: smaller bundle, 60fps, proper functionality.",
      "tasks": [
        "1. Run build and measure bundle size (target: <500KB)",
        "2. Performance test: 60fps during idle at all LOD levels",
        "3. Performance test: 30fps minimum during rapid pan/zoom",
        "4. Test on mobile device (iOS Safari or Chrome Android)",
        "5. Memory profiling: ensure no leaks during navigation",
        "6. Document before/after metrics",
        "7. Update CLAUDE.md with deck.gl-specific architecture notes",
        "8. Clean up any remaining R3F references in documentation"
      ],
      "performanceTargets": {
        "bundleSize": "<500KB gzipped",
        "idleFPS": ">=60",
        "interactionFPS": ">=30",
        "viewportQuery": "<5ms",
        "memoryGrowth": "0 during 5min navigation session"
      },
      "successCriteria": [
        "Bundle size <500KB",
        "60fps idle, 30fps+ interaction",
        "No memory leaks",
        "All features working",
        "Documentation updated"
      ]
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-MIGRATE-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "deck.gl Foundation - Install deck.gl and create DeckCanvas with OrthographicView",
      "passes": true,
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Installed deck.gl@9.2.5, created DeckCanvas.tsx with OrthographicView, toggle button in navbar. Commit: eed2f65",
      "blockedBy": null
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-MIGRATE-002",
      "category": "architecture",
      "passes": true,
      "description": "Layer Migration - Convert R3F layers to deck.gl layers",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-MIGRATE-001",
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Created MacroLayer, MesoLayer, MicroLayer. Commit: 7ef9708"
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-MIGRATE-003",
      "category": "architecture",
      "passes": true,
      "description": "LOD System - Create CompositeLayer with semantic zoom",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-MIGRATE-002",
      "notes": "COMPLETE: LOD crossfade transitions with 0.15 hysteresis."
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-MIGRATE-004",
      "category": "architecture",
      "passes": true,
      "description": "Interaction & State - Integrate deck.gl with @mms/toolkit",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-MIGRATE-003",
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Hover/click handlers integrated with interaction slice."
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-MIGRATE-005",
      "category": "architecture",
      "passes": true,
      "description": "Feature Parity - Restore all R3F features in deck.gl",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-MIGRATE-004",
      "notes": "COMPLETE: PathLayer for dependency lines, sidebar/timeline sync."
    },
    {
      "id": "DECK-MIGRATE-006",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 99,
      "description": "Cleanup - Remove R3F dependencies and optimize",
      "passes": false,
      "supersededBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-001",
      "blockedBy": "DECK-MIGRATE-005",
      "notes": "SUPERSEDED: Replaced by DECK-COMPLETE-001 through DECK-COMPLETE-005."
    },
    {
      "id": "STATE-ARCH-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "priority": 99,
      "description": "High-Performance State Architecture (R3F-specific)",
      "passes": false,
      "deprecated": true,
      "supersededBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-003",
      "notes": "DEPRECATED: Designed for R3F. deck.gl has built-in optimizations. RBush now in DECK-COMPLETE-003."
    },
    {
      "id": "PERF-002",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 99,
      "description": "Viewport Culling & Spatial Indexing (R3F-specific)",
      "passes": true,
      "deprecated": true,
      "supersededBy": "DECK-COMPLETE-003",
      "notes": "DEPRECATED: Foundation done for R3F. RBush for deck.gl in DECK-COMPLETE-003."
    },
    {
      "id": "D3-MIGRATE-001",
      "category": "architecture",
      "passes": true,
      "description": "D3 modules integration",
      "notes": "COMPLETE: d3-array, d3-time, d3-time-format, d3-scale, d3-interpolate integrated."
    },
    {
      "id": "URGENT-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "passes": true,
      "description": "React hooks audit",
      "notes": "COMPLETE"
    },
    {
      "id": "PERF-001",
      "category": "performance",
      "passes": true,
      "description": "Performance monitoring tool",
      "notes": "COMPLETE: r3f-perf implemented (will need deck.gl equivalent after migration)"
    },
    {
      "id": "TECH-DEBT-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "passes": true,
      "description": "Camera state migration to @mms/toolkit",
      "notes": "COMPLETE: Viewport state migrated to external store."
    }
  ],
  "completedFeaturesReference": {
    "description": "All other features (F001-F028, F101-F124, BUG-001 to BUG-009, HARNESS-*, etc.) remain completed and valid. See previous features.json backup for full list."
  },
  "categories": {
    "architecture": "Core architecture and migration tasks",
    "performance": "Speed and optimization",
    "ui": "User interface elements",
    "harness": "Development infrastructure"
  },
  "testingProtocol": {
    "description": "Mandatory testing sequence for each feature (see docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md)",
    "sequence": [
      "1. TYPECHECK: bun run typecheck",
      "2. VISUAL TEST: Playwright MCP screenshot",
      "3. CONSOLE CHECK: No errors",
      "4. INTERACTION TEST: Pan/zoom/LOD",
      "5. PERFORMANCE CHECK: 60fps idle, 30fps interaction",
      "6. MARK COMPLETE: passes:true"
    ]
  },
  "completionCriteria": "Project is complete when ALL DECK-COMPLETE-* features have passes:true"
}

Pronto! O harness foi reiniciado com sucesso.

## Resumo do que foi feito:

### Backups criados em `docs/archive/`:
- `features.json.bak-20251219-230100` (14KB)
- `claude-progress.txt.bak-20251219-230100` (94KB)

### Novos arquivos criados:

**`claude-progress.txt`** - Contém:
- Tarefa atual: DESIGN_PLAN
- Descrição consolidada da tarefa (com todos os ajustes do chat original)
- Sub-tasks organizadas
- Pain points do RFQ listados

**`features.json`** - Contém 10 features sequenciais:
| ID | Descrição |
|---|---|
| DESIGN-001 | Backup existing docs to docs/archive/ |
| DESIGN-002 | Research and collect visual references |
| DESIGN-003 | Create DESIGN_SPEC.md - Visual Mood & Moodboard |
| DESIGN-004 | Create DESIGN_SPEC.md - Design System |
| DESIGN-005 | Create DESIGN_SPEC.md - Problem-Solution Matrix |
| DESIGN-006 | Create DESIGN_SPEC.md - Screen Specifications |
| DESIGN-007 | **Planning View with Sandbox Mode (PRIMARY)** |
| DESIGN-008 | Create DESIGN_SPEC.md - Component Specifications |
| DESIGN-009 | Create DESIGN_SPEC.md - Data Structures |
| DESIGN-010 | Create DESIGN_SPEC.md - Interactions & Implementation |

Todas as features estão com `passes: false`, prontas para serem executadas na outra sessão.
